name: Sittuyin
author: AGP
description: Sittuyin (Burmese chess) on an 8x8 board. Only the pawns (Ne) start on the board, in a staggered split formation; the players then alternately DEPLOY their other eight pieces (King, General, 2 Chariots, 2 Elephants, 2 Horses) onto their own half (chariots on the back rank). The General (Sit-ke) is a ferz (one step diagonally); the Elephant (Sin) moves one step diagonally or one step straight forward; the Horse is a knight and the Chariot a rook. The signature 'sit-tu' promotion lets a pawn on one of the two long diagonals (in the enemy half) promote to a General -- but only if that player's General has already been captured. Win by checkmate; stalemate is a draw. White (player 0) deploys and moves first.
